subject: Anthropology
title: High-Altitude Adaptation
description: A fast breakdown of High-Altitude Adaptation for builders.

hook.text: At 14,000 feet, most people gasp for air. Tibetans breathe normally. Their genes have adapted to thin air.

buildup.text: Tibetans carry a variant of the EPAS1 gene that prevents overproduction of red blood cells at high altitude.

discovery.text: Andean peoples adapted differently — with larger lungs and more hemoglobin. Same problem, different genetic solutions.

twist.text: The Tibetan EPAS1 variant came from Denisovans. Ancient interbreeding gave a survival edge thousands of years later.

climax.text: High-altitude adaptation shows evolution in action. Humans haven't stopped evolving — the environment still selects.

punchline.text: Evolution didn't stop — it just got local.

quiz.question: Where did Tibetans get the EPAS1 gene variant?
